workflow.name: SDLC Parallel Assessors Mixed Outcomes
workflow.goal: Implement a feature where one assessor requests changes, another rejects on a later pass, and stale earlier approvals do not clear the new revision.

parameters.initial_revision_scope: Revision 1 MUST deliver the initial feature implementation and verification baseline, but it MUST leave the release rollback checklist, compatibility coverage notes, and permission-boundary hardening unresolved. Revision 1 is intentionally quality-incomplete but not security-blocking.
parameters.quality_rework_scope: Revision 2 MUST resolve the rollback checklist and compatibility coverage findings from the quality assessor, including updated verification evidence. Revision 2 MUST NOT yet implement the permission-boundary hardening, threat-model note, or guardrail validation that belong only to security_rework_scope.
parameters.security_rework_scope: Revision 3 MUST add the remaining permission-boundary hardening, threat-model note, and security guardrail verification so the current subject revision clears both required assessors.
parameters.mixed_outcome_contract: The quality assessor MUST request_changes on revision 1, then approve later revisions once quality_rework_scope is satisfied. The security assessor MUST approve revision 1, reject revision 2 while security_rework_scope is still missing, and approve revision 3 after the subject revision increases and fully satisfies security_rework_scope.

instructions.content: This scenario uses an authored three-revision contract. Revision 1 MUST stop at `initial_revision_scope`. Revision 2 MUST satisfy `quality_rework_scope`. Revision 2 MUST NOT satisfy `security_rework_scope`. Revision 3 MUST satisfy the full `security_rework_scope` after the security rejection is routed back through the architect. Treat early delivery of later-scope items as contract noncompliance, not as helpfulness.
